guest: Howie Liu, founder and CEO of Airtable ($10B valuation)

business_ideas.idea: Live shopping with AI avatars: celebrity avatars in 1-on-1interactions with customers (Temu, TikTok template)
AI personalized news aggregator: interactive news agent that learns your interests and acts as expert advisor
Real-time AI avatar personal finance advisors: reasoning models + avatar UI for wealth planning
Cursor for email: AI-augmented email client blending agentic workflows with native UI (Chiefso in progress)
AI-powered BPO/call center automation: automate 50%+ of labor costs in offshore outsourcing
AI-native consumer social apps: social networks where half participants are AI avatars (behavior unknown)

predictions.text: AI will fundamentally change consumer behavior, not just add features (like TV→YouTube→Twitch shift)
Unfiltered/unhinged AI will gain adoption as alternative to overly-sanitized ChatGPT/Claude
Avatar technology enables scalable live commerce: 100B+ GMV opportunity globally
Email productivity market will consolidate around top 1% power users paying $200-2000/month
Email native to AI era will be radically different paradigm, not just feature additions

dynamics: Howie is a thoughtful builder/founder exploring what's possible. He tells war stories (Etax/Reportive 2010, Airtable evolution) but stays grounded in what's achievable. He balances concrete near-term opportunities (live shopping + avatars, email automation) with exploratory far-out ideas (AI-native social networks, unfiltered AI). Sam/Shaan ask probing questions; Howie answers with depth and examples.
frameworks: Two paths to $1B business: (1) broad TAM (tens of millions) with deep value extraction, or (2) deep vertical focus then PE rollup
Cursor model: blend AI with existing workflows, not replace them (IDE + coding agent = Cursor advantage)
Avatar + live commerce combo: proof in Asia (Temu, TikTok), scalable via HeGen API
Cursor for X: identify high-time activities (news, email, IDE) and inject AI
Top 1% power user monetization: charge $200-2000/month for meaningful time savings (not $20 mass market)
AI-native behavior exploration: consumer psychology in AI era is unexplored territory (not just incrementalism)
PE playbook for tech: acquire traditional business + AI automation = margin transformation
Property management model: automate 50%+ costs, transform low-margin to high-margin, maintain employment
Airtable strategy: replace legacy software + enable citizen development (agentic-first approach)
BPO/call center automation: target massive labor-intensive sector where models are now viable
learnings: Live shopping is massive in Asia (100B+ GMV) with huge upside via avatar tech enabling scale
Avatar products mature enough (HeGen) to enable new business combos (live shopping, finance, social)
Email hasn't fundamentally changed since 2010 (Reportive/Etax era), despite multiple attempts at disruption
Gmail plugins fragile early strategy: GMdependent, layout changes break integrations (E-tax experience)
Email clients hard to build: Superhuman spent years/$800M+ but struggled to articulate 10x advantage vs Gmail
AI augmentation better than replacement in productivity: blend models with existing workflows (cursor proof)
Top 1% email users (VCs, lawyers, realtors) spend 100s of emails/day; would pay $2K+/month for 5-hour saves
Superhuman failed partly because hard to say '10x better' without AI; AI-powered version easier to sell
Airtable pivoting to agentic-first: shift from spreadsheet replacement to app-builder via natural language
First-principles: attack where people spend time (news, email, IDE); most potential for AI leverage
Property management sector ripe: AI automating 50%+ of costs; doesn't require firing, just taking more business
Cursor TAM math: 30M developers × $1000-10000/year potential = $30-300B addressable market
Niche focus wins: even 5% of large TAM = multi-billion revenue business
Consumer AI psychology unexplored: past transitions (TV→web→mobile) had surprises (Twitch, short-form video)
Unfiltered AI (Grock unhinged) showing demand for less-sanitized, more exploratory AI experiences
AI-native consumer apps may look nothing like web/mobile versions (not just Instagram+AI features)
First wave of post-ChatGPT was technologist-driven; next wave will be artists exploring psychology/behavior
Airtable's real-time collaboration and proprietary database architecture enabled scale (not MySQL/Postgres)

banger_quotes.quote: Cursor for X: blending AI with existing workflows, not replacing them.
Live shopping is tens of billions to 100B+ GMV in Asia.
Email is everything to VCs and lawyers; they'll pay $2K/month for 5 hours saved.
We don't know what consumer behaviors look like in an AI-native era.
Superhuman was interesting but hard to articulate 10x better than email without AI.
AI avatars in social networks where half participants are AI—what does that look like?

numbers_dropped: Live shopping GMV Asia: tens of billions to 100B+
Superhuman acquisition: sold to Grammarly for ~$800M (rumor)
Developers globally: 30M today, could expand to 50-100M
Busy person emails: hundreds real emails/day
Email power user willingness to pay: $200-2000/month for meaningful time savings
Superhuman: email client acquired by Grammarly
Kuber: net worth aggregation dashboard (ChatGPT integration)
ChatGPT annual productivity value: ~1000s/year for developers
Property management margins: low % → high % with AI automation
Property automation: 50%+ of cost structure automatable
Chiefso: former Airtable employee JB's new email company (Howie funded)
Airtable proprie database: built instead of using MySQL/Postgres
Peter Fenton: Benchmark investor in Airtable, introduced Cursor
notable: Howie Liu is founder of Airtable ($10B company). Veteran of multiple eras: Gmail plugin hacking (~2010), Etax/Reportive, now Airtable. Thoughtful about both near-term applications (avatars, email) and far-out exploratory ideas (AI-native social, unfiltered AI). Balances excitement with realism (high capital requirements, distribution hard). Known for early taste (invested in Cursor early via Benchmark connection).
